task_summary: Context-specific, druggability-filtered drug-target nomination from CD4+ T cell Perturb-seq

steps.title: Acquire and validate inputs
steps.description: Download the MIT-licensed analysis tables from github.com/emdann/GWT_perturbseq_analysis_2025: gene-level DE estimates (DE_stats.suppl_table.csv), cluster annotations (clustering_results_and_annotations.csv), condition-specificity scores (nde75ntotal50_cluster_specificity.csv), autoimmune-enrichment results, guide KD efficiency, and the bundled druggable-class gene lists (kinases/GPCRs/ion-channels/enzymes/transporters/nuclear-receptors) plus ClinVar/GWAS lists. Load each, print shapes/columns, confirm perturbation identifiers and the three stimulation conditions (Rest/Stim8hr/Stim48hr). Save a manifest.csv documenting every input, its source URL, and row/column counts. Checkpoint the merged long-format DE table as de_long.parquet.

steps.title: Define a pathogenic pro-inflammatory target program
steps.description: Assemble a defensible 'disease-relevant state' signature to score perturbations against — a pro-inflammatory / Th1-Th17 effector program (e.g. IFNG, IL17A/F, TNF, CXCL8, TBX21, RORC, STAT3-network effectors) drawn from the paper's own cytokine/polarization gene sets and standard immunology references. Document gene membership and provenance in a signature table. Render a heatmap of signature-gene DE across the top perturbations per condition as the first QC figure (program_signature_heatmap.png).

steps.title: Score directional, context-specific perturbation effects
steps.description: For each perturbed regulator in each condition, compute a signed program-shift score (how far knockdown moves cells toward vs. away from the pathogenic program) using the gene-level DE estimates, plus a context-specificity index contrasting stimulated (Stim8hr/Stim48hr) vs Rest. Flag regulators whose effect is stimulation-conditional (acts only in activated, disease-relevant cells). Require cross-donor/cross-guide robustness using the guide-KD and robustness fields. Output perturbation_scores.csv and a volcano/quadrant figure (perturbation_directionality.png) with knockdown-attenuates-inflammation vs knockdown-amplifies quadrants labelled.

steps.title: Add human-genetics support
steps.description: Anchor candidates to human disease genetics WITHOUT re-deriving the paper's cluster-enrichment: for each candidate regulator query Open Targets (via the clinical-genomics connector / GraphQL) for association scores across the 14 autoimmune diseases, and cross-reference the bundled GWAS-catalog and ClinVar pathogenic lists. Add columns for max autoimmune association score, top disease, and genetic-evidence class. Save genetics_annotation.csv and a figure ranking candidates by combined causal-effect × genetic-evidence (genetics_support.png).

steps.title: Add druggability and tractability
steps.description: Classify every candidate by protein family using the repo's druggable-class lists, then query ChEMBL (bioactivity connector) and Open Targets tractability buckets for small-molecule / antibody tractability, known ligands, and clinical-phase compounds. Produce a druggability_annotation.csv with tractability tier and best-known modality per candidate, and a dot plot (druggability_landscape.png) of candidates positioned by causal effect size vs tractability tier.

steps.title: Assess clinical novelty
steps.description: Query ClinicalTrials.gov and ChEMBL mechanism/indication data to label each candidate as (a) approved/clinical-stage target for an immune indication, (b) drugged for a non-immune indication (repurposing angle), or (c) novel/undrugged. This is the differentiation axis — it separates rediscoveries from genuinely new nominations. Save clinical_novelty.csv and a summary bar/stacked figure (novelty_breakdown.png).

steps.title: Integrate into a ranked target shortlist
steps.description: Join all evidence layers into one table and compute a transparent composite nomination score with four explicit, separately-visible components (directional causal effect, context-specificity, genetic evidence, druggability), keeping clinical-novelty as an annotation not a score inflator. Produce target_shortlist.csv (all candidates, all evidence columns, ranked) and a headline figure (top_targets_evidence_matrix.png) — a heatmap/table of the top ~20 nominations with each evidence layer as a column. Sanity-check that known positives (e.g. STAT3-network, CTLA4) surface where expected.

steps.title: Deep-dive the top novel nominations
steps.description: For the 3–5 highest-ranked NOVEL (undrugged or non-immune-drugged) candidates, write a short mechanistic vignette each: causal signature, condition-specificity, downstream program, genetic evidence, tractability, and a one-line therapeutic hypothesis (agonize vs antagonize, and in which disease). Ground each claim in the retrieved data/literature. Render per-target mini-panels (top_target_vignettes.png).

steps.title: Assemble reproducible pipeline and report
steps.description: Consolidate the analysis into a clean, rerunnable notebook (target_nomination_pipeline.ipynb) and write findings_report.md (methods, what differs from the preprint, the shortlist, the novel vignettes, and explicit limitations — chiefly that nominations are computational hypotheses requiring wet-lab validation). Save all artifacts, embed key figures, and provide the shortlist CSV as the primary deliverable.

desired_outputs: Target-nomination figure set (scatter/dot plots, directionality maps)

feasibility.rationale: All required inputs are small MIT-licensed CSVs (gene-level DE estimates 5.3 MB, cluster annotations, condition-specificity, autoimmune enrichment) downloadable from the analysis repo, so the full pipeline runs locally without the 22 M-cell matrix or a cluster. The three added layers (directionality, druggability/tractability, clinical novelty) are non-overlapping with the preprint and map directly onto connected resources (Open Targets, ChEMBL, ClinicalTrials.gov). Main uncertainty is that no wet-lab validation is possible, so credibility rests on multi-evidence triangulation rather than a single score.
